sections.title: What belongs here
sections.summary: Tower defense, auto-battlers, horde modes, survivor-like systems, and other games where pressure arrives in timed waves and the player manages spatial or economic responses.

sections.title: Core structural rule
sections.summary: Separate schedule, spawn, economy, and combat resolution. These systems interact tightly, but they should not become one monolithic game loop.

sections.title: Preferred project structure
sections.summary: - wave schedule and encounter definitions - spawn and pathing systems - economy and reward systems - build/deploy or placement systems - combat and status resolution - telemetry an

sections.title: Source-of-truth boundaries
sections.summary: Canonical data should usually include: - wave definitions - spawn compositions - reward and economy tables - unit/tower/loadout definitions - encounter pacing rules

sections.title: Verification model
sections.summary: Unit tests matter, but long-run scenario simulation matters more. Temper should encourage: - encounter simulations across many seeds - economy progression checks - dominant-strateg

sections.title: Release and hotfix concerns
sections.summary: Small value changes can shift the entire difficulty curve. This family needs release notes that explain pacing impact, not just implementation impact. Before shipping: - compare pr

sections.title: Failure modes
sections.summary: - wave content authored with no reusable pacing language - spawn logic intertwined with rendering or UI - economies that scale faster than pressure or vice versa - runaway dominant

sections.title: Team operating notes
sections.summary: Separate content and engine ownership clearly. One contributor can own encounters, one economy, one combat runtime, one UI, but only if simulations and dashboards expose the cross-

sections.title: Best Temper overlays
sections.summary: - `Kaplan 🎯` for fairness and early trust - `Meier ♟️` for build diversity and tradeoffs - `Wright 🌱` for systemic interaction - `Miyamoto ✨` for readability under pressure
